channel.title: Comments for page "Groups Of Interest"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Groups Of Interest"

item.title: dado on the GOI Hub
item.content:encoded: So, dado already has a hub (page for what is dado hub yes) and I feel like someone just needs to touch up the hub a little and put it on the GOI page because he is a group of interest and the POI list is defunct. All i am saying is to just put the dado hub on the GOI page. Am I allowed to do this myself?

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
bartman2, instead of posting multiple comments in succession, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

Or would it be better to just wait?

I don't know if any Daevite hubs are currently in the works. If you really really really want to see that GoI on this list, maybe ask around and see if a hub is in development. If not, ask if anyone is interested in doing so/what would be needed to get a hub written up in a way that (hopefully) the majority of Daevite writers can agree with.

item.title: Re: The Daevites
item.content:encoded: Or would it be better to just wait?

item.title: Re: The Daevites
item.content:encoded: should they just be contacted and asked to make a hub?

item.title: Re: The Daevites
item.content:encoded: If you want to know, go check the "daevite" tag on O5 and check all the works posted. You will find the names of the authors in the "History" section at the bottom of the page (look at the iteration 0, it's the one when the author posted the thing).

item.title: Re: The Daevites
item.content:encoded: Well, who's contributed a lot to the Daevites?

item.title: Re: The Daevites
item.content:encoded: Generally authors who have written for the GOI/contributed significant lore. A couple of the older hubs were written as part of a contest many years back, however.

item.title: Re: The Daevites
item.content:encoded: who makes the hubs for each group?

item.title: Re: The Daevites
item.content:encoded: That's what I was thinking. A hub here at least.

item.title: Re: The Daevites
item.content:encoded: The Daevites can get an entry if a hub is created. That's really the only requirement, since they have a tag already.

item.title: The Daevites
item.content:encoded: I feel like the Daevites have been around long enough and have been important enough for them to get their own entry. Anybody agree?

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Addressing some information here…

There is a guide for newbies. If I remember correctly it's a "must read" before creating your account.

The Guide for Newbies is required reading for anyone who wants to be a mainsite member. For readers who aren't interested in having a wikidot account or joining the mainsite/interacting with the mainsite community, there isn't a super pressing need to read that page.

Link: http://www.scp-wiki.net/guide-for-newbies
Author: DrClef

General note, while DrClef created the page and posted the first iteration of it, it's become more a product of staff as a group. The page has many edits from other staff who have updated the page over the years to do things like trim down wordcount bloat, update policy information, and add links.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
I know this may sound off topic but I was reading through the comments and I saw yours, I mean, you are everywhere, amazing.

And yes it's as Zyn says. There is a guide for newbies. If I remember correctly it's a "must read" before creating your account.

You are the one in charge of looking for reviewers.

Even it is harsh the first time I must say it's a good learning experience.

Link: http://www.scp-wiki.net/guide-for-newbies
Author: DrClef

item.title: Re: GoI idea - The Nest
item.content:encoded: If you want reviews on something, try to reach out to people yourself. Information on that here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews

item.title: Re: GoI idea - The Nest
item.content:encoded: Could you help me try to redirect people to it please?

item.title: Re: GoI idea - The Nest
item.content:encoded:
If you're asking about the writing process, it's suggested you make a post on the Idea Crit forum so you can get advice on the general concept. After two qualified critters express approval of the idea (symbolized by a "greenlight"), you make a post in the Draft Crit forums to show off what you've written and get further critique.

This process is detailed fully in How To Write An SCP (even if you decide to write a tale instead of an SCP) in the "Posting" tab.

item.title: Re: GoI idea - The Nest
item.content:encoded: How would I go about doing that? Would I just write then post the tale?

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
the-ever-shifting, instead of posting multiple comments in succession, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

ETA: also, this kind of sounds like a Serpent's Hand splinter group, with the quirk that they really dislike the CI specifically for some reason.

item.title: Re: GoI idea - The Nest
item.content:encoded:
We're not all sadists. A GOI with explicitly "benevolent" interests could theoretically work, but the issue is that "pure good" and "pure evil" are boring concepts from a literary perspective. There's a certain level of complexity that's required for readers to be invested.

Again, easiest way to create a GOI and demonstrate its appeal is to write an article featuring them.

item.title: Re: GoI idea - The Nest
item.content:encoded: Honestly, seeing how some people react to 'humane' things on this wiki, I expect this to fall flat on its anomalous face. But, in the mean time, I'll keep working on it.

item.title: Re: GoI idea - The Nest
item.content:encoded:
To give anomalous entities that have no I'll will towards reality a chance to live a relatively 'normal' life. This mainly came into mind after going through an interview that I was going to make for an SCP humanoid I was going to make, where she states that "most of us never chose to be anomalous."

As for why readers would be invested? Dunno. Maybe as a temporary relief from all the agencies that are out there to eliminate the anomoalous. Maybe knowing that there's a group that protects anomoalous humanoids and sentients will ease something in them? I don't know.

Honestly, seeing how some people react to 'humane' things on this wiki, I expect this to fall flat on its anomalous face. But, in the mean time, I'll keep working on it.

item.title: Re: GoI idea - The Nest
item.content:encoded:
Easiest way to get the ball rolling on a GOI is to write a tale/SCP featuring them.

The basic idea is an ancient group whose primary purpose is to protect anomalous humanoids, sentients, and sapients that pose a medium to nonexistent risk to the Veil, normalacy, and reality as a whole.

To what end and for what reasons? Why would readers be invested in this GOI?

item.title: GoI idea - The Nest
item.content:encoded:
Please note tat this is merely an IDEA for a GoI. If anyone gets interested. send me a message and we can get started on building it out.

The basic idea is an ancient group whose primary purpose is to protect anomalous humanoids, sentients, and sapients that pose a medium to nonexistent risk to the Veil, normalacy, and reality as a whole. While not against the Foundation and Other GoIs, they dislike the way they treat sentient, sapient, and humanoid anomalies.

They will, at times, help the Foundation to secure and contain dangerous anomalies and humanoids that are a direct threat to the Veil and reality, or who want to see reality be destroyed or the world thrown into chaos. Potentially, they could also be the keepers of certain SCPs.

They are severely opposed to the Insurgency, and believe that the group should be eradicated as soon as possible.

If an anomalous entity is tied to an anomalous object, then the Nest will also try and contain the object.

Again, this is just an idea. If interested, contact me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh..I'd have to disagree. The contrast between formal and informal style is kinda jarring for me. But to each their own I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It adds to the charm, I say leave it be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am I the only one who thinks that the description for Atari Arcadia is way too informal in comparison to the rest of the page?

Unless I'm missing something with the lore of this particular GOI, I highly doubt any researcher would say that a legitimate anomalous organization is "still out there, snorting pentagrams of cocaine off joysticks to this day."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you to Agente Shuffle for drawing the new GRU-P logo and Polymehan_odysseu for getting the ball rolling on a more unique GRU-P Logo.

item.title: Re: Entry Request
item.content:encoded: Are you one of the authors for it?

item.title: Entry Request
item.content:encoded: Can I set up a hubpage for PENTAGRAM ?, Since it has a tag and 16 pages.

item.title: Admin Post
item.content:encoded: Approved!

item.title: Group of Interest Entry Request
item.content:encoded:
• SCP-1070 - Re-Educational Software by Roget
  • SCP-2600 - The Empty Box by Roget
  • SCP-4564 - Butthole Surfers by Dr Balthazaar
  • SCP-4605 - E.T the Earthen Totality by Popsioak
  • SCP-5020 - InfestStation by Deadly Bread
  • SCP-5032 - PantherQuest by ihpkmn
  • SCP-5200 - The Brown Box by Roget
  • SCP-5599 - fish golf by Roget
  • Ballad Of The Fifth Arcadian by Roget
  • My Father The Commodore:
    • Business Is War by Roget
    • For The Masses, Not The Classes by Roget
    • Goodbye by Roget
  • Group of Interest Hub

12 articles by 5 authors, plus a hub and tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't Pentagram qualify for an entry at this point? The only requirement they're missing is a hub page.

item.title: Children of the Night
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't the Bigfoot be a GOI by now? They may not be the most organized, but they have plenty of articles, authors, and a tag. All they're missing is a hub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No matter how may times I do it, I always find myself rereading this entire page with just as much excitement as the last time. Dull day at work or just a cozy evening, this is usually my go-to. Quick and fun. It's insane how this community has so much creativity and imagination for world-building of the SCP Universe. I always play out fictional scenarios in my head as I read each GOI blurb lol. I remember when there were only a handful of GOIs when it first started and now look at it! I freaking love this.

item.title: Re: GoI Idea: Anomalous Protectors Patrol
item.content:encoded:
Are you trying to solicit writers to make articles based on your idea, or are you trying to get feedback on an idea you want to write?

If it's the first thing, please don't do that. Write your own articles about the GOI and other people will join you if they like what they see.

If it's the second thing, that's what the ideas critique forum is for.

item.title: GoI Idea: Anomalous Protectors Patrol
item.content:encoded:
Description: APP (Anomalous Protectors Patrol) is essentially an organization of anomalous humans who use their powers to protect people who don't have powers, like superheroes. The GoI was started in the late '60s by eight individuals with anomalous properties, who are known as the Champion's League. Many members have arcane & occult abilities and some even have reality-bending capabilities. The organization poses no threat to the Foundation itself, but the Foundation still keeps a close eye on them just to make sure.

I know about the whole X-Man syndrome that people don't like, but I still feel like this organization's existence would make sense in the SCP universe because some people would try to use this new "gift" to help people around the world.

Any thoughts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the "So, You Have An Idea For A Group Of Interest" section, the "Basics" part had this :

The GoI must have been issued a searchable site tag. More information on requesting tags can be found here

But the link is a link to the first Tag Suggestion Thread and we had this one since last year. Wouldn't it be more relevant to replace the link of the old thread by the link to the actual thread ?

item.title: Moderator Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
From this very page:

So, You Have An Idea For A Group of Interest.
hide

Basics

  • The GoI must feature in a minimum of five positively-rated mainsite pages with at least three authors contributing.
  • The GoI must be open to contributions from all site members.
  • The GoI must have been issued a searchable site tag. More information on requesting tags can be found here
  • The GoI must have a hub page or equivalent, which will be linked to from the GoI hub and the top bar.

Submitting
Make a new post on the GoI Hub discussion page documenting that the group meets the above requirements. An administrator will respond, either approving the GoI or denying it with a provided reason. If denied, the GoI can be re-submitted after the reason(s) are addressed. Administrators will have the final decision on the acceptance of new groups of interest.
Examples of reasons a group may be denied include deletion-range rated articles or if a GoI is derivative of an existing one. If any piece is not within deletion range, the community has decided that the piece fits within the SCP Foundation paradigm.
If a GoI only meets the minimum requirements, it is possible that group may not strike a chord with the community and will need additional work to flesh out its full potential and place in the site-wide worldbuilding. Should a group fall below the minimum requirements, it may be removed after a review by Site Administration.
Remember
It may take several months to create a Group of Interest. While a new GoI can be submitted at any time, writers are encouraged to emphasize quality of content over speed of implementation, to make the in-progress GoI a stronger contender for acceptance.
Groups of Interest should inspire community members to write articles outside of the original seed. Community buy-in is critical to the success of these creations.
Any questions regarding GoI submission can be asked in comments on this page or via wikidot message to a staff member.

Also, don't double post. Edit your previous comment.

item.title: Another one but better.
item.content:encoded:
“The king's little men do the work for the king! No man is spared from the horrors of war! We call ourselves the kings zealots! We may never disobey! We must fight for salvation or you must die with devastation! No heresy is allowed let the king take the crowd! We are the kings salvation Everyman in our path will die with devastation, you fight with the best you’ll die like the rest!”

Overview: “The kings little men” is a secretive group like a secret service lurking on every street. No matter who you are, they could take you for you to be a subject, no one knew where they came from. It seems they are new coming from 2017. They’re mission is to take the foundation into a haven of subjects using scps as tools, they are hostile to everyone. Any contact with them that is a clear threat will be kill on site, no mercy will be given to any of their combative members.

Departments that are known:

Combative: they average members on their side usually raiding and taking.

Logistics: They will be on every mission to take and bring back usually not hostile.

item.title: Here is an idea.
item.content:encoded:
Universal Inc!

Universal Inc
Main idea: Universal inc is an organization capable to take and sell planets, they would also make space craft and high tech weapons and items, they are very open anyone capable to buy is welcomed getting in the business is hard, they would also colonize and work with other species, they work with the 1% and help out with MC&D..

More will be added when I think of more.

item.title: Re: official request for Three Moons Initiative being an official GoI thingamajig
item.content:encoded: Added to the list!

item.title: Re: official request for Three Moons Initiative being an official GoI thingamajig
item.content:encoded:
Note that the code for this would be:

 [[div class="content-panel standalone series" style="background-color: #FFFFFF;"]] + [[[Three Moons Initiative Hub|The Three Moons Initiative]]] [[f>image http://scpsandbox2.wdfiles.com/local--files/daveyoufool/timicon.jpg]] **Overview:** The Three Moons Initiative is an extradimensional human military organization based in SCP-2922-C, an afterlife also known as Corbenic. Through a partnership with the Corbenese deity JALAKÅRA, they act as a multi-dimensional security force for the protection of the human race. While Initiative personnel believe that they're operating in humankind's best interests, military overreach, poor communication, issues with internal bureaucracy, and other factors — combined with their disproportionately high military strength to our own — have made them a potential liability to public safety in our dimension. As such, they maintain an unstable peace with the Foundation. Operatives of the Three Moons Initiative are to be treated with extreme caution. To see all documents tagged with //three-moons-initiative//, [/system:page-tags/tag/three-moons-initiative click here]. [[div style="text-align:right"]] ,,[#toc Back to Top],, [[/div]] [[/div]]

item.title: official request for Three Moons Initiative being an official GoI thingamajig
item.content:encoded:
It meets the requirements now: tag, more than 5 establisehd articles, different authors — and now, the hub page.

…if you don't mind.

Edit: Aw fack, forgot the blurb. Sorry. Here we gooooo.

The Three Moons Initiative

Overview: The Three Moons Initiative is an extradimensional human military organization based in SCP-2922-C, an afterlife also known as Corbenic. Through a partnership with the Corbenese deity JALAKÅRA, they act as a multi-dimensional security force for the protection of the human race.

While Initiative personnel believe that they're operating in humankind's best interests, military overreach, poor communication, issues with internal bureaucracy, and other factors — combined with their disproportionately high military strength to our own — have made them a potential liability to public safety in our dimension.

As such, they maintain an unstable peace with the Foundation. Operatives of the Three Moons Initiative are to be treated with extreme caution.

To see all documents tagged with three-moons-initiative, click here.

Back to Top

item.title: Re: Shark Punching Center
item.content:encoded: Addressed, thanks!

item.title: Re: Shark Punching Center
item.content:encoded:
Typo in the SPC section:

though some documentation fails to differentiate between "selachian"and "aquatic".

There should be a space after "selachian".

item.title: Re: Parawatch
item.content:encoded:
Entry added!

…hopefully I didn't bork the coding this time.

Also… tech team, maybe get some collapsibles on this page?

item.title: Parawatch
item.content:encoded:
Seeing as Parawatch has hit full tag requirements (and has gone higher), here's a blurb for the bunch:

Parawatch

Overview: The Parawatch Wiki is an online web forum of conspiracy theorists, paranormal enthusiasts, and amateur writers, operating with the intent of investigating and exposing anomalous phenomena. The group primarily compiles short stories on their forum, detailing encounters with paranormal phenomena, historical cases, and any unusual events users have experienced. Further operation and any potential impact on normalcy is hindered by the geographic spread of the userbase and the lack of central coordination.

Despite investment in the paranormal, Parawatch has no knowledge on the nature of anomalous phenomena, the Veil, and the Foundation's existence. This in tandem with public obscurity has prevented them from potentially endangering the Veil. The group is currently being left active as a means of misinforming and misleading other investigations into the anomalous.

To see all documents tagged with parawatch, click here.

Back to Top

+ code
- code
 [[div class="content-panel standalone series" style="background-color: #FFFFFF;"]] + [[[Parawatch Hub|Parawatch]]] [[f>image http://scp-wiki.wdfiles.com/local--files/watts-maxwell-personnel-files/rook6.png]] **Overview:** The Parawatch Wiki is an online web forum of conspiracy theorists, paranormal enthusiasts, and amateur writers, operating with the intent of investigating and exposing anomalous phenomena. The group primarily compiles short stories on their forum, detailing encounters with paranormal phenomena, historical cases, and any unusual events users have experienced. Further operation and any potential impact on normalcy is hindered by the geographic spread of the userbase and the lack of central coordination. Despite investment in the paranormal, Parawatch has no knowledge on the nature of anomalous phenomena, the Veil, and the Foundation's existence. This in tandem with public obscurity has prevented them from potentially endangering the Veil. The group is currently being left active as a means of misinforming and misleading other investigations into the anomalous. To see all documents tagged with //parawatch//, [/system:page-tags/tag/parawatch click here]. [[div style="text-align:right"]] ,,[#toc Back to Top],, [[/div]] [[/div]]

item.title: Re: i think this is the right place
item.content:encoded: Rereading this i can see how it would seem cartoonish I shouldve elaborated more. That said anarchist terrorism is a real world thing and was more prevalent at the turn of the century. A loose organization of cells causing terror and chaos as a way to weaken the establishment. Maybe im alone in thinking a group like that armed with scp's would be a cool concept to explore.

item.title: Re: i think this is the right place
item.content:encoded:
is there a GoI that is a terrorist like organization, as in they use the scp's to cause general chaos and panic?

Given that "causing general chaos for no reason" is kind of a shallow goalset, I would say no, there's no GoI dedicated specifically to that kind of thing. Splinter groups and individuals, sure. But that kind of group comes off more as a cartoony villain sort of premise. Someone can totally check me if I'm wrong, though.

item.title: i think this is the right place
item.content:encoded: I think this is the right place to ask but is there a GoI that is a terrorist like organization, as in they use the scp's to cause general chaos and panic? I know the Chaos Insurgency has chaos in the name but they seem different than what I mean

item.title: Re: dado consideration.
item.content:encoded: I agree with COzer here but don't know if it's allowed.

item.title: dado consideration.
item.content:encoded: Considering there is no Persons of Interest section, would it be worthwhile to include dado in this hub? its a rather fun series of articles and I'd love to see it highlighted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Typo addressed; thank you for posting!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Typo in the IJAMEA entry: "and actively seek to exploit the various anomalies" should be "seeks".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that the Ursus Maritimus incident actually exists on the site, it may be pertinent to include a link to it in the WWS blurb?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops, totally missed that. Thanks for bringing that to staff attention, and thanks to ARD for fixing that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just noting that the Deer College entry appears to have been added incorrectly inside of the above entry, so it looks a bit wonky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Entry added!

Hmm… the page is getting rather long. We made need some collapsibles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now that Deer has a tag, I'm gonna go ahead and request it gets put on this page - the image does need to be rehosted on the page itself, but nothing else needs to change afaik

Deer College

Overview: Deer College is a coeducational liberal arts and occult sciences college in the heart of scenic Three Portlands, an extra-dimensional city-state located adjacent to the American Northweast and Southern England; with a curriculum that focuses on the marriage of mundane and occult disciplines and a well-regarded Anart program, Deer is often seen as the liberal arts counterpart to its GOC-sponsored rival across town, ICSUT Portlands. A number of prominent figures in the anomalous world are Deer alumni, including Vincent Anderson, the CEO of Anderson Robotics, and Esther "lesbian_gengar" Kogan, one of the co-founders of Gamers Against Weed.

As the Foundation's operations in Three Portlands are heavily restricted by a number of agreements with the FBI's Unusual Incidents Unit, Deer College itself is relatively free of Foundation intervention; however, all Deer alumni are automatically marked as persons of interest, and may be subject to increased Foundation scrutiny.

To see all documents tagged with deer-college, click here.

Back to Top

code under here
more like beer college amirite
 [[div class="content-panel standalone series" style="background-color: #FFFFFF;"]] + [[[*http://www.scp-wiki.net/deer-college-hub|Deer College]]] [[f>image http://www.scp-wiki.net/local--files/deer-college-hub/reddeer.png width="60px"]] **Overview:** [[[about-deer|Deer College]]] is a coeducational liberal arts and occult sciences college in the heart of scenic [[[three-portlands-hub|Three Portlands]]], an extra-dimensional city-state located adjacent to the American Northweast and Southern England; with a curriculum that focuses on the marriage of mundane and occult disciplines and a well-regarded Anart program, Deer is often seen as the liberal arts counterpart to its GOC-sponsored rival across town, ICSUT Portlands. A number of prominent figures in the anomalous world are Deer alumni, including Vincent Anderson, the CEO of Anderson Robotics, and Esther "lesbian_gengar" Kogan, one of the co-founders of Gamers Against Weed. As the Foundation's operations in Three Portlands are heavily restricted by a number of agreements with the FBI's Unusual Incidents Unit, Deer College itself is relatively free of Foundation intervention; however, all Deer alumni are automatically marked as persons of interest, and may be subject to increased Foundation scrutiny. To see all documents tagged with //deer-college//, [/system:page-tags/tag/deer-college click here]. [[div style="text-align:right"]] ,,[#toc Back to Top],, [[/div]] [[/div]]
